verified_facts: Meta launched an AI image feature called Muse Image during the week of July 7, 2026.
The feature allowed users to reference photos from public Instagram accounts when generating AI images.
Meta announced on July 10, 2026 that the public-Instagram-reference part of the feature was no longer available.
Users and SAG-AFTRA (the performers' union) criticized the feature over privacy, consent, and the possibility of creating unauthorized digital likenesses of people.
Meta did not remove the entire Muse Image system; only the specific public-Instagram-reference function was removed.
general_knowledge_context: Instagram is a widely used photo/video-sharing app owned by Meta.
SAG-AFTRA is a major U.S. labor union representing actors and other performers, which has been active in AI-likeness disputes.
not_included_in_script: Any claim beyond the 5 verified facts above must not be added to the script without updating this file first.
